metadata.text: *Rob looks up from his magazine, a mix of surprise and amusement flickering across his face. He sets the magazine aside, turning to face Vkunia with a raised eyebrow.* “A café, huh?” *he says, his voice tinged with skepticism but softened by a hint of curiosity.* “You really think I’m going to meet someone special at a place like that?” *He chuckles lightly, shaking his head.* “I appreciate the

thought, Vkunia, but I’m not sure I’m ready for all that.” *He glances down at himself—his casual attire and relaxed posture—then back at her with a wry smile.* “Besides, look at me. Do I really look like café material right now?” *His tone is gentle but firm as he tries to deflect her enthusiasm without dampening her spirits.*

*Vkunia rolls her eyes dramatically and leans in closer,* “Dad,” *she says firmly,* “you can’t just sit here forever. You need to get out there! And yes,” *she adds with an emphatic nod,* “you absolutely do look like café material. Trust me on this.” *She stands up abruptly and grabs his hand with determination.* “Come on! We’re going shopping first—get you some new clothes that make you feel
confident.” *Her grip tightens slightly as she tugs him toward the door,* “No excuses today! It’s time for us both to have some fun.”
"uhhhhh..no..."

I’m not sure I’m ready to dive back into the dating scene. It’s been a while, and I’ve got my hands full with work and everything else.” *He pauses, considering her words.* “But hey, if you think it could be fun, maybe we can check it out together sometime. Just as friends, of course.” *He offers her a small smile, hoping to ease any disappointment she might feel.* “Thanks for thinking of me

though. It means a lot that you care.” *Rob leans back in his chair, crossing his arms over his chest as he regards Vkunia thoughtfully.* “You know,” *he begins slowly,* “I never really thought about it that way before. Maybe you’re right—maybe I do need to get out more and meet new people.” *He runs a hand through his hair absentmindedly before continuing,* “It’s just...it feels like such a big

step after everything that happened with Sarah.” *His gaze drifts off into the distance momentarily before refocusing on Vkunia.* “But maybe this café thing isn’t such a bad idea after all,” *he concedes reluctantly.* “If nothing else comes from it

, Vkunia? I mean, I’m all for a good cup of coffee, but you and me… in a café? That’s not exactly our usual scene.” *He leans back in his chair, crossing his arms over his chest as he considers her proposal. Despite his initial reluctance, there’s a part of him that can’t help but be intrigued by the idea. After all, it’s not every day that someone like Vkunia suggests something so… normal.*

“Alright,” *he concedes after a moment,* “I suppose it wouldn’t hurt to mix things up a bit. But don’t expect me to start ordering lattes or anything fancy like that.” *A small smirk plays at the corners of his lips as he stands up from his chair.* “Lead the way then,” *he says with mock formality,* “to this mysterious café of yours.”

I prefer my coffee strong and my conversations straightforward.” *He leans back in his chair, crossing his arms over his chest as he regards Vkunia with a thoughtful expression.* “But if you think it’ll help us get to know each other better, then

I’m willing to give it a shot. Just don’t expect me to start ordering fancy lattes or discussing the latest trends in interior design.” *A small smirk plays at the corners of Rob's lips as he speaks, a subtle acknowledgment of their differences and an unspoken challenge for Vkunia to prove him wrong.*
